_meta.purpose: Deterministic anti-slop sanitizer seed data — the moat gate. Engineers seed these arrays into the global_library layer (app-wide, read-only). The sanitizer runs a code-detectable pass over generated content and HARD-FAILS on any kill_word, banned_phrase, em-dash density breach, curly-quote, or (under editorial register) register_gated_phrase / structural_pattern.
_meta.rationale_for_layer: These are app-wide constraints identical for every org and member. They are NOT per-org or per-member — they belong in the read-only seed DB the sanitizer loads at startup. Org/member layers MAY append additional banned vocabulary (e.g. competitor names, off-brand words) but the universal floor here is shared.
_meta.extraction_method: Verbatim output of parseAntiSlop() run against the canonical file on 2026-06-20. Counts are ground-truth: the running sanitizer parses these exact strings at runtime. kill_words=70, banned_phrases=28, register_gated_phrases=11.
_meta.extraction_note: The sanitizer PARSES the canonical markdown at runtime so it never drifts. This JSON snapshots that parse for a DB seed. If engineers seed from JSON instead of parsing markdown, they MUST keep this file in sync with anti-slop-universal.md — the canonical file is the single source of truth. See sanitizer-spec.md § Runtime-Parse Design.

matching_rules.kill_words: whole-word, case-insensitive (\b<word>\b, regex-escaped). Multi-token entries (e.g. 'that being said', "here's the thing:") match as exact whole token sequences.
matching_rules.banned_phrases: case-insensitive substring (lower.includes(phrase)). No word boundary — a substring match anywhere in the text fails.
matching_rules.register_gated_phrases: whole-word, case-insensitive (\b<phrase>\b). Boundary matching is deliberate so 'act now' never fires inside 'react now' / 'impact now'. ONLY active under register==='editorial'.
matching_rules.em_dash_max_per_paragraph: count of '—' (U+2014 EM DASH) per paragraph (paragraphs split on /\n\s*\n/). > max => violation.
matching_rules.curly_quotes: any presence of ‘ ’ “ ” in the text => one violation per distinct character found.
matching_rules.structural_patterns: S1-S5 regex/heuristic detectors. HARD-FAIL only under register==='editorial'; advisory WARNING otherwise. S6-S7 are taste-level, NOT mechanized.

kill_words._categories_in_source: verbs (16), adjectives (16), nouns/abstract (7), transitions (subset, 9), filler (22) — flattened into one array by the parser; category preserved below in kill_words_by_category for engineers who want UI grouping.

kill_words_by_category._note: Original source taxonomy preserved for UI grouping / suggestion tooltips. The 'use_instead' suggestions come verbatim from anti-slop-universal.md replacement tables (lines 24-81). Nouns and filler have no per-word replacement in source.

transitions_in_source_NOT_captured_by_pa._note: These appear in the Transitions table of anti-slop-universal.md (lines 67-82) but the parser DROPS them: 'it is worth noting that' (>3 words after no-strip), 'in the realm of' / 'in the landscape of' (>3 words), 'in today's [anything]' (contains '['). They are NOT enforced as kill_words. If engineers want them enforced, add as banned_phrases (substring match) — see net_new flag in manifest.

replace_instructions_in_source_NOT_enfor._note: anti-slop-universal.md line 239 prescribes replacements that the parser does NOT enforce (the filler section regex only captures single ^[a-z]+$ tokens). Document-only guidance, not gate-enforced. Engineers MAY add the left-hand strings as banned_phrases if they want enforcement.

banned_phrases._categories_in_source: opening (7), transitional (5), closing (7), chatbot artifacts (5), pseudo-profound flourishes (4). Parser strips trailing '...' and [placeholders], keeps stable prefix; entries with placeholders like 'the future of x is y' keep literal 'x'/'y' tokens.
all: the future of x is y
once you do x, y feels wrong
after x, you'll never go back to y

pseudo_profound_flourishes: the future of x is y
once you do x, y feels wrong
after x, you'll never go back to y

register_gated_phrases._rationale: Legitimate direct-response urgency/scarcity CTAs for ads + sales pages. Off-register ONLY for editorial-restraint brands (VOICE.md register: third-person-editorial / density: editorial-restraint). Silent for ads/sales/default. Code-enforced half of the DR Register Map (05-marketing-demand/copywriting/_index.md).

structural_patterns._note: S1-S5 are mechanized in detectStructuralSlop(). HARD-FAIL under register==='editorial', advisory WARNING otherwise. S6-S7 are taste-level and NOT mechanized (enforced by hostile-editor critic + slop grader). S8 is a candidate, not yet detectable. Detection logic is verbatim from sanitize-copy.mjs lines 270-382.

patterns.description: "THE X is THE Y" aphorism — billboard, not a person talking.
patterns.detection: For each sentence with word-count <= 12, regex /\bthe\s+[\w'’-]+\s+is\s+the\s+[\w'’-]+/i. Sentence-scoped, short only, to limit false positives.

patterns.description: Contrast-reframe family — the soft cousins of the banned Pattern 7 ("X isn't Y. It's Z."). Ban the whole family. Manufactured-insight tell.
patterns.shipped_offender_example: Posts in your voice, not a ghostwriter's. / Knowledge that compounds, not resets. / not just impressions / a specific mechanic rather than a line on a feature grid
patterns.detection: For each sentence, ANY of these 4 regexes (first match wins, one S2 per sentence): (a) /,\s+not\s+(?:just\s+|a\s+|an\s+|the\s+)?[\w'’-]/i  '", not Y" trailing contrast'; (b) /\bnot\s+just\b/i  '"not just X"'; (c) /\b(?:is\s+)?never\s+(?:the|a|an|just)\b/i  '"is never the/a Y"'; (d) /\brather\s+than\b/i  '"X rather than Y"'.

patterns.shipped_offender_example: Outbound that survives the AI flood. / Content that sounds like you wrote it / The knowledge layer that compounds.
patterns.detection: Headline-scoped only. For each headline candidate, regex /^[\w'’-]+(?:\s+[\w'’-]+){0,3}\s+(?:that|who)\s+[\w'’-]+/i.

patterns.description: Imperative punchy-pair CTA — two consecutive imperative sentences (one terse) = ad cadence, not a person.
patterns.shipped_offender_example: Bring your setup. Leave with a plan for it.
patterns.detection: For each adjacent sentence pair (i, i+1): first word of BOTH is in the IMPERATIVE_VERBS set AND min(wordcount(a), wordcount(b)) <= 6. Skips one index after a match (no double-count).

patterns.description: Rule-of-N aphoristic fragment headline — 2+ spelled cardinal number-words in a short headline. Digits are deliberately EXCLUDED (a numeral is usually a real metric, e.g. '8 to 12 pieces').
patterns.shipped_offender_example: Four parts, one memory. / Three services. One operator. One context.
patterns.detection: Headline-scoped, word-count <= 7. Count matches of /\b(?:one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten)\b/gi. If matches >= 2 => finding.

patterns.description: Colon-aphorism: [short setup]: [punchy payoff]. TASTE-LEVEL — NOT mechanized (regex cannot separate a slop colon-aphorism from a legitimate label colon).
patterns.shipped_offender_example: Ask any engineer: read access only. / The bar: send the same sequence to 500 companies.
patterns.detection: NOT mechanized. Enforced by hostile-editor critic (critique-pass-protocol.md § M) + slop grader.

patterns.description: Balanced "clever" declarative — short, witty, rhythmically balanced. Reads like a billboard, not a person mid-conversation. TASTE-LEVEL — NOT mechanized.
patterns.detection: NOT mechanized. Enforced by hostile-editor critic + slop grader, anchored to negative examples.

S8_candidate.description: Meta-labels that name the rhetorical move as a heading ("The problem" / "The stakes" / "The fix"). Essay-scaffolding-as-UI. Held with S6/S7 for the hostile-editor critic until a reliable detector exists.
S8_candidate.note: Builder/structural slop bypasses ALL string gates (2026-06-16) — these arrive as component structure (eyebrow labels, crossheads), not copy strings, so the sanitizer is blind to them. Must be caught at app-building Website Build Mode structural review, not the copy gate.

imperative_verbs_set._note: Used by S4 detection. First word of an imperative sentence. Verbatim from sanitize-copy.mjs lines 63-118 (IMPERATIVE_VERBS Set).

number_words_set._note: Used by S5 detection (spelled cardinals). Verbatim from sanitize-copy.mjs lines 119-130 (NUM_WORDS).
